companies.name: Chipper Cash
companies.whySummary: Laid off ~30% of staff and CTO departed, March 2026
companies.sourcingAngle: Engineering org just absorbed a 30% headcount cut and lost its CTO in the same quarter — remaining staff are likely covering wider scope with less certainty about leadership direction. Good moment to pitch LemFi's stability and clear ownership structure.

events.description: Chipper Cash lays off approximately 30% of remaining staff amid continued restructuring.
CTO departs Chipper Cash after 4 years, no replacement named.
Reports of a bridge round at a reduced valuation following delayed Series D close.

companies.name: N26
companies.whySummary: Hiring freeze reported, missed European expansion targets
companies.sourcingAngle: No confirmed layoffs, but a hiring freeze plus missed expansion targets suggests internal pressure on delivery teams. Frame outreach around scope and ownership rather than instability.

events.description: N26 misses European expansion targets for second consecutive quarter.
Internal memo reportedly announces hiring freeze to focus on profitability.
VP of Product exits N26 for a role at a competitor.

companies.name: Klarna
companies.whySummary: Laid off 700 employees, ~10% of workforce, June 2026
companies.sourcingAngle: A 10% workforce reduction this recently means displaced engineers and designers are actively job-hunting right now, before they land elsewhere. Move fast on this one.

events.description: Klarna lays off 700 employees, roughly 10% of workforce, citing AI-driven efficiency gains.
Glassdoor rating drops from 3.9 to 3.4 following layoff announcement, review volume up sharply.

companies.name: Brex
companies.whySummary: SMB product team dissolved following strategic pivot
companies.sourcingAngle: The SMB product team was dissolved outright rather than resized — those PMs and designers have no team to return to internally, making them unusually receptive to outbound right now.

events.description: Brex lays off 150 staff as it pivots away from the SMB market toward enterprise.
Employee reviews cite uncertainty about team restructuring and unclear reporting lines.

companies.name: Revolut
companies.whySummary: Minor cultural complaints only, no major signals
companies.sourcingAngle: Signal is weak here — this is a low-priority week for Revolut, worth revisiting after the next scan rather than leading outreach with any specific hook.

events.description: Minor regulatory scrutiny reported in one European market, no material impact disclosed.

companies.name: Wise
companies.whySummary: Insufficient data — no signals detected in latest scan

companies.name: Monzo
companies.whySummary: Head of Engineering departs, no layoffs detected
companies.sourcingAngle: A senior engineering leadership exit without any accompanying layoff signal often means the org is mid-reshuffle — worth a light-touch check-in with ICs on that team.

events.description: Head of Engineering departs Monzo for an external opportunity, internal backfill not yet announced.

companies.name: Flutterwave
companies.whySummary: Regulatory challenges reported across multiple African markets
companies.sourcingAngle: Regulatory pressure across several markets is creating uncertainty without an accompanying headcount cut — likely to unsettle compliance and risk teams specifically.

events.description: Flutterwave faces regulatory challenges in multiple African markets over licensing compliance.

companies.name: Zilch
companies.whySummary: Glassdoor rating declining, funding round delayed
companies.sourcingAngle: Declining internal sentiment plus a stalled funding round is a classic pre-layoff pattern — worth building relationships now, ahead of any public announcement.

events.description: Reports suggest Zilch's next funding round has been delayed amid investor caution in BNPL sector.
Glassdoor reviews increasingly cite concerns about job security and leadership communication.
